hypothesis: If ML-driven surrogates (eqV2 and eSEN) accurately predict energies, forces, and stresses to guide SSNEB, then the ML-SSNEB workflow will reproduce the minimum-energy pathways and transition states for CsPbI3 phase transitions and TiO2-B to anatase transformations with accuracy comparable to VASP-only SSNEB while reducing the number of SSNEB iterations and total computational time.
